The '''Light Festival''' was a holiday observed by the [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]] of [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]] on the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]] to honor the [[Tree of Light]] and the young warriors who had traveled to it to rejuvenate its powers with [[Light Dust]].

The young warriors received [[Silver Feather of Bravery|Feathers of Light]] to signify their participation in the rejuvenation. The Tree of Light was thought to keep the [[Night Spirit/Legends|Night Spirit]] at bay. Unfortunately, the enemy [[Dulok/Legends|Duloks]] agreed with this belief and have occasionally endeavored to destroy the Tree at this time of weakness to curry the favor of the Night Spirit.

The quest to rejuvenate the tree was originally reserved exclusively to young Ewok males of sufficient age. Princess [[Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka/Legends|Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka]] strongly objected to her exclusion because of her sex while [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket Wystri Warrick]] had parallel complaints about the required minimum age. As a result, both Ewoks, along with [[Latara/Legends|Latara]], secretly followed the quest group lead by Wicket's eldest Brother [[Weechee Warrick|Weechee]], until they proved their worth in their own ways well enough to join the group.

The enlarged group completed their expedition in the face of considerable Dulok opposition and the trespassers acquitted themselves so well that the Elders not only declined to punish them, but awarded them with Feathers as well. Presumably, the Festival's strictures were relaxed accordingly for a more inclusive policy.
